rule.name: Azure AD Multi-Factor Authentication Disabled
rule.description: The following analytic detects attempts to disable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for an Azure AD user. It leverages Azure Active Directory AuditLogs to identify the "Disable Strong Authentication" operation. This activity is significant because disabling MFA can allow adversaries to maintain persistence using compromised accounts without raising suspicion. If confirmed malicious, this action could enable attackers to bypass an essential security control, potentially leading to unauthorized access and prolonged undetected presence in the environment.
tags: OS: Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Cloud
rule.query:
`azure_monitor_aad` category=AuditLogs operationName="Disable Strong Authentication"
  | rename properties.* as *
  | rename targetResources{}.type as type
  | rename initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName as initiatedBy
  | rename userAgent as user_agent
  | fillnull
  | stats count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    BY dest user src
       vendor_account vendor_product user_agent
       initiatedBy signature
  | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
  | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
  | `azure_ad_multi_factor_authentication_disabled_filter`
false_positives: Legitimate use case may require for users to disable MFA. Filter as needed.
